source.work: Salgarella, E. 2020. Aegean Linear Script(s): Rethinking the Relationship between Linear A and Linear B. Cambridge: CUP. DOI 10.1017/9781108783477
source.extraction_method: Category-per-sign read from the cell shading of Tables 2-3 (pp. 35-36); shading extracted programmatically from PDF cell fill colours (dark blue = CMYK 0.495/0.287/0/0, 25 cells; light blue = CMYK 0.172/0.084/0.003/0, 27 cells) and cross-checked against the rendered page images. Category key quoted verbatim from p. 34. AB-number/value correspondences only as printed in her Index of Signs Cited (pp. 412-13).

scheme.label: homomorphic
scheme.definition_quote: The above results are summarised in Tables 2–4, with LA-LB homomorphic syllabograms highlighted in light blue, while homomorphic and also likely homophone syllabograms in dark blue.

scheme.label: homomorphic and also likely homophone
scheme.definition_quote: sign homomorphy is likely to imply homophony, at least in some instances and with an approximation
scheme.page: 32 (category realised as the dark-blue cells of Tables 2-4, key p. 34)

scheme.label: AB (shared 'core')
scheme.definition_quote: LB = AB (shared ‘core’) + B (subset: a language-specific component) … The former are called ‘AB’ signs since shared with LA and, although in LB these signs were slightly modified, comparable variants are still detectable.

scheme.label: B (subset: a language-specific component)
scheme.definition_quote: The latter represent new introductions in LB and appear to be language-bound (Greek only).

scheme.label: B-subset innovation categories (syllabic subset)
scheme.definition_quote: New signs introduced in the B-subset belong to the following categories: a) o-series: do, jo, mo, no, qo, so, wo b) e-series: pe c) complex/doublets: a2, ra3, ro2, dwe, dwo, two, pte d) undeciphered: *19, *63, *64, *83, *89

scheme.label: Index tripartition: Signs shared between Linear A and B / Linear A-only signs / Linear B-only signs
scheme.definition_quote: Signs shared between Linear A and B … Linear A-only signs … Linear B-only signs

shared_sign_identity.as_printed: High percentage of shared signs (72% sign identity)
shared_sign_identity.note: her n. 90 credits Steele and Meissner 2017 for the figure

new_lb_introductions.as_printed: some twenty signs are taken as new introductions in LB

core_syllabary_without_la_parallel.as_printed: In the core syllabary seven o-series signs and pe do not seem to have parallel in the LA

additional_syllabary_without_la_parallel.as_printed: in the additional syllabary this is the case for three out of the four additional a-vowel sounds (a2 /ha/, a3 /ai/, ra3 /rai/) and some labialised (dwe, dwo, twe, two) and yodised (ro2, pte?) signs

cypriot_stable_signs.as_printed: These signs are: a, i, da (corresponding to ta in the Cypriot Syllabary), na, pa, po, ro (corresponding to lo in the Cypriot Syllabary, but the sign represents both ro/lo in LB), sa, se, ti, to.

gorila_numbering.as_printed: In GORILA V simple signs (‘signes simples’) are numbered AB 01–87, A 100–371, A 400VAS–418VAS … [composite signs] have conventionally been numbered A 501–664 in GORILA (‘signes complexes’) and are unique to LA (with one exception, MA+RU, retained in LB)

table2_shading_tally_DERIVED_NOT_PRINTED.note: tallied by us from cell fills; Salgarella prints no numeric totals for the shading

table3_shading_tally_DERIVED_NOT_PRINTED.note: tallied by us from cell fills; no printed totals

lb_undeciphered_syllabograms_DERIVED_NOT.note: row count of Table 4 as printed

position_quotes.quote: sign homomorphy is likely to imply homophony, at least in some instances and with an approximation. A number of arguments have been put forward in support of the validity of this equivalence, which, however, have to be used with due caution.

as clearly pointed out by Steele and Meissner, in doing so we try to explain an earlier situation (i.e. LA phonology) with a later one (i.e. LB phonology), which is not a methodologically straightforward procedure. Nevertheless, the overall weight of the arguments provided would favour the reliability of applying this method, thus allowing to read LA with at least approximate phonetic values.
i) phonetic continuity, in view of the legitimacy of the backward projection of LB sound values;
Although most scholars see this as the most plausible scenario (esp. Steele and Meissner 2017), we cannot be entirely sure this was always the case.

position_classification.rationale: She argues for the transfer with four explicit arguments (pp. 32-3: context of adaptation, shared sequences/name equations, logogram retention + acrophony, Cretan-Cypriot sign stability) and asserts 'legitimacy of the backward projection' (p. 374), but flags the backward-projection direction as 'not a methodologically straightforward procedure' (p. 34) and concedes 'we cannot be entirely sure this was always the case' (p. 374 n. 2). She defends; she does not test against held-out data.

lexical_endorsements.quote: LA and LB share a number of sign sequences, often interpretable as toponyms or anthroponyms (e.g. toponyms pa-i-to ‘Phaistos’, se-to-i-ja, su-ki-ri-ta; anthroponyms da-i-pi-ta, pa-ra-ne)

lexical_endorsements.item: qa-qa-ru → LB qa-qa-ro; di-de-ru → LB di-de-ro
lexical_endorsements.quote: LA anthroponyms qa-qa-ru and di-de-ru turned into LB qa-qa-ro and di-de-ro … These name equations are a strong piece of evidence providing reasonably secure readings of the homomorphic signs contained in the sequences.
this sign is a monogram made up with the syllabograms MA and RU and likely to represent the Minoan word for wool (maru/malu), borrowed into Greek as μαλλός ‘wool/fleece’
This sign has been shown to be the first syllable of the supposedly Minoan word for ‘fig’ nikyleon … and was therefore read as ‘ni’ by the acrophonic principle.

not_graded_repo_tokens.PA3: No pa3/AB-number correspondence printed anywhere; her Index lists AB *56 with NO value (p. 412) and Table 4 (p. 37) carries *56 as an LB undeciphered syllabogram. anchors.csv has no ab_number for PA3 either. pending_primary: searched Tables 2-4 (pp. 35-37), §4.1.4.1 (p. 291), Index of Signs Cited (pp. 412-16).
not_graded_repo_tokens.ZU: No zu value printed; her Index lists AB *79 with NO value (p. 413) and Table 4 (p. 37) carries *79 as undeciphered. anchors.csv has no ab_number for ZU. pending_primary: same search as PA3.

notes.JU_tension: Table 2 (p. 35) prints 'ju?' as a light-blue (homomorphic) core-syllabary cell, but Table 4 (p. 37) lists AB *65 among 'LB undeciphered syllabograms' and the Index (p. 412) gives 'AB *65' with no value. Graded here from Table 2 as printed, marks 'ju?'.
notes.A3_tension: a3 is shaded light-blue (homomorphic) in Table 3 (p. 36), yet p. 38 counts a3 among the a-vowel signs without LA parallel; her n. 102 (p. 38) notes 'a3 was perhaps already attested by the time of Shaft Grave IV, and therefore an LA source cannot be excluded'. a3/ai is not a repo anchor token.
notes.WE: WE is not among the repo's 59 anchor tokens; for the record her Table 2 (p. 35) shades we light blue (homomorphic).
